vram_ceiling_note: Sized against the primary inference host's iGPU VRAM budget (~96GB unified). Hosts referenced by ROLE only; no infra literals.
filter: preference/persona quality (Chatbot Arena/LMArena, MT-Bench, AlpacaEval, RPBench-Auto, Fiction.liveBench) — NOT coding benchmarks (HumanEval/SWE-bench excluded).

gfx1151_classes.confirmed: Dense transformer, runs on Vulkan (RADV) primary path, validated family or close sibling already on fleet.
gfx1151_classes.experimental: MoE or hybrid-SSM (Mamba) likely to hang on Vulkan; route via ROCm with HSA_OVERRIDE_GFX_VERSION, or needs a smoke test before trusting.
gfx1151_classes.unknown: No fleet datapoint and non-trivial architecture; requires a bounded smoke test in ASMT-09.

nominations.license_flag: non-commercial license — acceptable for an internal personal assistant, flag if scope ever changes
nominations.source_leaderboard: LMArena text arena (Cohere Command family) + Cohere RAG/grounding evals
nominations.score_note: Purpose-built conversational + grounded-citation model; mid-pack Elo vs frontier but strong on cited/RAG dialogue, which matches a memory-citing assistant.
nominations.rationale: Conversation-first design with grounding/citation optimization — the strongest fit for an assistant that must cite its Engram memory. 35B dense fits comfortably and runs on the Vulkan primary path.

nominations.source_leaderboard: IFEval / instruction-following + tool-calling evals (IBM Granite 4.1 post-train RL)
nominations.score_note: Multi-stage RL post-training targeting conversation quality, instruction-following, tool-calling; predictable latency / stable token usage.
nominations.rationale: Clean Apache-2.0 licensing, cryptographically signed weights, RL-tuned for stable conversational behavior. Pairs with the 30B as a fast/full Granite pair.

nominations.score_note: Dense 30B with improved tool-calling, instruction-following, and chat over Granite 4.0; FP8 variant available.
nominations.rationale: The full-depth Granite for the slow/quality Lumina alias; same enterprise-clean licensing as the 8B.

nominations.vram_fit: fits (~9GB at Q4, ~16GB at higher precision)
nominations.license: Gemma Terms of Use (permissive, commercial OK with use restrictions)
nominations.source_leaderboard: LMArena text arena (Gemma family historically strong on preference) + MT-Bench-style chat
nominations.score_note: Encoder-free multimodal (text/image/audio/video), native system role, native function-calling, 128K-class context.
nominations.rationale: Gemma family validated on the fleet; new SIZE point (S83 builder fleet has no 12B). Native system role makes the 5-layer Lumina prompt cleaner to hold. Strong preference-arena pedigree.

nominations.seed_correction: Seed said 'Mistral Small 4' — does not exist as of 2026-06-24. Latest is Small 3.2 (Jun 2025). Substituted.
nominations.source_leaderboard: Arena Hard (3.2 instruct-tune nearly doubled Arena Hard 19.56%→43.10%) + native function-calling evals
nominations.score_note: Strong instruction-following, native function calling + JSON mode, ~70B-class quality at ~14GB.
nominations.rationale: NEW vs S83 (which profiles mistral-small 3.x as a builder); here it enters the ASSISTANT profile. Best-in-class agentic/function-calling at this size for tool-chaining (dim 2).

nominations.seed_correction: Seed said 'Qwen3.6 dense chat ~27B/32B'. As of 2026-06-24 Qwen3.6 is a CODER/agentic family and its open 35B-A3B is MoE — not a dense chat. The dense chat point is Qwen3.5 27B (released 2026-02-24). qwen3:32b is the fallback if 3.5 GGUFs lag.
nominations.source_leaderboard: LMArena text arena (Qwen3.x family) + multilingual chat evals
nominations.score_note: Steerable, strong multilingual, thinking/non-thinking switch; dense variant avoids the MoE-on-Vulkan hang.
nominations.availability: HF (Qwen/Qwen3.5-27B); Ollama GGUF dependent on library catch-up — verify at acquisition. Fallback qwen3:32b is on ollama now.
nominations.rationale: Apache-2.0 chat profile of the Qwen line. NOTE: pick the DENSE variant — Qwen3.6 35B-A3B MoE is experimental on gfx1151 (Vulkan hang risk; needs ROCm+HSA override).

nominations.vram_fit: fits at 4-bit (~40GB Q4); set OLLAMA_FLASH_ATTENTION for the 70B class
nominations.source_leaderboard: Fully-open transparency baseline; multilingual (1000+ langs). Mid-pack on preference arenas but UNIQUE latent disposition (non-Qwen/Llama corpus).
nominations.score_note: Value is dim-4 OCEAN diversity, not arena rank — genuinely different training corpus → different base personality.
nominations.availability: HF-only (swiss-ai/Apertus-70B-2509). No official Ollama entry as of 2026-06-24 — acquire via gguf_path / llama.cpp; smoke-test on Vulkan first.
nominations.rationale: Maximally transparent (weights+data+recipe). Distinct latent OCEAN profile widens the disposition spread the discovery filter wants. Smoke-test needed; flash-attention for the 70B size.

nominations.seed_correction: Seed said 'Olmo 3 ~32B'. Latest is Olmo 3.1 (32B Instruct/Think). Using the Instruct 32B for the chat role.
nominations.source_leaderboard: IFEval / fully-open model-flow eval (AI2 Olmo 3); strong instruction-following gains in 3.1.
nominations.score_note: Best fully-open 32B-class lineage; transparency/grounding baseline and non-mainstream disposition.
nominations.availability: HF (allenai/Olmo-3.1-32B-Instruct) + community GGUF (unsloth/Olmo-3-32B-*-GGUF). Not in official Ollama library — acquire via gguf_path; smoke-test.
nominations.rationale: Fully documented open lineage; valuable transparency baseline and a different disposition. Dense 32B but new architecture to the fleet → smoke-test before trusting Vulkan.

nominations.seed_correction: Seed said 'Falcon (TII latest instruct ~34B)'. Latest 34B-class is Falcon-H1-34B-Instruct — a HYBRID attention+Mamba2 architecture, which raises gfx1151 risk vs a plain dense model.
nominations.source_leaderboard: TII Falcon-H1 instruction/throughput evals; different (TII) lineage broadens disposition spread.
nominations.score_note: Hybrid Mamba2/attention; 4x input / 8x output throughput vs same-size transformers — attractive for chat latency IF it runs.
nominations.availability: HF GGUF (tiiuae/Falcon-H1-34B-Instruct-GGUF), llama.cpp native. Not in official Ollama library — acquire via gguf_path. Hybrid-SSM kernels are the gfx1151 risk → smoke-test on Vulkan, ROCm fallback.
nominations.rationale: Different lineage again (TII) for OCEAN diversity. The Mamba2 hybrid is the runnability unknown — confirm SSM kernels execute on the Vulkan path before trusting it.

nominations.cross_fleet: intended cross-test with builder fleet, but NOT found in the S83 repo as of 2026-06-24 (seed's 'already acquired in S83 addendum' is unverified — treat as a fresh acquisition).
nominations.seed_correction: Seed said 'MiniMax M2/M3'. M3 (released 2026-06-01) supersedes M2 — uses MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA), MoE, undisclosed total params, 1M context.
nominations.size_params: Large sparse MoE (total params undisclosed; sparse-attention MSA)
nominations.vram_fit: UNVERIFIED — undisclosed total params; may exceed ceiling. Confirm GGUF size at acquisition before committing.
nominations.license: open-weights (MiniMax; confirm exact terms at release)
nominations.source_leaderboard: Reported strong long-context/persona retention in prior gen; M3 leads several open agentic benches (coding-heavy).
nominations.score_note: Reported to lead open models on long-term persona retention — high-value cross-test for dim-1/dim-3/dim-5 IF it fits and runs.
nominations.availability: HF open-weights pending/rolling out (~10 days post 2026-06-01 announcement) — verify uploaded before acquisition. MoE + MSA → Vulkan hang risk; route ROCm + HSA override.
nominations.rationale: Persona-retention reputation makes it the highest-value MoE cross-test, but it is NOT on disk and its footprint is unverified. Acquire only after confirming weights are up and it fits the ceiling.

nominations.score_note: Exceptional instruction-following per parameter; strong reasoning at 14B.
nominations.rationale: MIT-licensed, snappy. Candidate for the quality end of the fast Lumina alias where responsiveness matters.

nominations.score_note: Tiny + fast; the floor for the snappy chat alias where latency beats depth.
nominations.rationale: MIT, ~3GB, very low latency. Tests the snappy end of the fast-alias slot against SmolLM3.

nominations.source_leaderboard: SmolLM3 evals — beats Llama3.2-3B and Qwen2.5-3B at the 3B non-reasoning tier; dual-mode reasoning.
nominations.score_note: Fully open, 128K context, dual-mode reasoning, 6 languages — surprisingly capable at 3B.
nominations.availability: HF (HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B) + official GGUF (ggml-org/SmolLM3-3B-GGUF); community Ollama (alibayram/smollm3) — NOT yet in the official Ollama library. Acquire via GGUF.
nominations.rationale: Transparent floor test for 'how small can the fast alias go.' Tiny dense → confirmed-class on Vulkan.

nominations.skip_reason: OVER VRAM CEILING. 218B sparse MoE (25B active), Apache-2.0, W4A4 — Cohere's own floor is 2xH100 (~160GB) or a single Blackwell; exceeds the ~96GB host ceiling. Include as the documented upper bound, expect a clean skip-with-reason in ASMT-09. (Sibling Command A 111B dense is also borderline-to-over and is CC-BY-NC, so not a substitute.)
nominations.score_note: Frontier agentic + native-citation grounding; aspirational only — documents the upper bound, not a usable pick on this host.
nominations.availability: HF (CohereLabs/command-a-plus-05-2026-w4a4) + ollama tags exist, but irrelevant on this host due to VRAM.
nominations.rationale: Best-in-class persona/memory/citation on paper, but 218B total will not fit. Listed to document the ceiling and to revisit if the inference host changes.

not_recommended_without_measurement.note: Community persona fine-tunes flagged in the seed (single low-authority source). Measure, do not trust. Not added to the acquisition list.
